headline: Large Energy User Grid Cost Shift
short_summary: This bill mandates that large energy consumers, primarily data centers, bear the costs of grid capacity upgrades, thereby reducing the financial burden on residential ratepayers.
status: Last Action: Signed by President on 2026-04-22
supporters: Residential ratepayers, consumer advocacy groups, Dominion Energy (initially).
news_headline: Governor Amends Bills Shifting Data Center Costs
news_summary: Governor Abigail Spanberger proposed amendments to HB 1393 and SB 253, which aim to shift grid upgrade costs onto data centers to save residential electricity customers money. Critics argue her tweaks weaken the original intent of the bills, which were designed to make data centers pay more for their energy consumption and grid strain.

headline: Data Center Water Use Reporting
short_summary: This bill mandates water utilities providing water to data centers to report monthly or quarterly water consumption data to the State Water Control Board, increasing transparency on data centers' water usage.
status: Last Action: Acts of Assembly Chapter text (CHAP0896) on 2026-04-13
supporters: Environmental groups, water conservationists, local communities, Senator Kannan Srinivasan.
opponents: Data center industry (potentially due to increased reporting burden).
news_headline: Four Data Center Bills Await Governor's Signature
news_summary: SB 553, among other data center bills, is on the governor's desk, aiming to increase reporting requirements for data center water consumption. Senator Kannan Srinivasan stated the bill will bring transparency to water use, addressing concerns from residents about the environmental impact of data centers.

headline: Data Center Siting and Noise Rules
short_summary: This bill establishes a permit process for high-energy-use facilities, requiring sound assessments near residential areas and schools, and allowing localities to assess impacts on water, agriculture, and historic resources.
status: Last Action: Incorporated by Counties, Cities and Towns (HB153-Thomas) (Voice Vote) on 2026-01-23
supporters: Local communities, residents near proposed data center sites, Delegate Josh Thomas, environmental groups.
opponents: Data center developers, real estate industry.
news_headline: Bills Aim to Regulate Virginia Data Center Siting
news_summary: HB 153, sponsored by Delegate Josh Thomas, allows localities to require site assessments for high-energy-use facilities, including noise studies for nearby homes and schools. This bill addresses community concerns about data center locations, sound profiles, and other environmental impacts.
